is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum infections in children treated with chlorproguanil-dapsone in Tanzania.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports dhfr codons 51, 59, 108 and dhps codons 436, 437, 540, 581 — all curated target positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives numerator/denominator per village: DHFR Ile51-Arg59-Asn108 Kwevihombo 15/42, Skimu 21/55, before; DHPS Ser436-Gly437-Glu540 15/41, 18/52; plus Gly581 counts (3/50 before, 2/40 after in Skimu).

sub_country_location.evidence: Frequencies reported per village (Kwevihombo, Skimu) in the East Usambara Mountains, northeast Tanzania — sub-national resolution.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Samples collected April 1999–June 1999 (single window well within 3 years).

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from asymptomatic children in villages in northeast Tanzania.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical drug trial with molecular genotyping of field samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples collected as part of a bednet trial preintervention survey; references prior related trials (ref 7 1995, ref 15) but this is a distinct 1999 collection.

supplement.evidence: Per-locus frequencies present in Table 1 of main text; no supplementary material required.

assessment.summary: Chlorproguanil-dapsone treatment trial in asymptomatic P. falciparum-infected children in villages (Kwevihombo, Skimu) in the East Usambara Mountains, NE Tanzania, April–June 1999. Reports dhfr (51,59,108) and dhps (436,437,540,581) genotype prevalences with extractable counts per village before/after treatment. Meets all criteria.
